response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is primarily a TypeScript/Node.js web engineer with a strong background in GIS data collection platforms and distributed HTTP services, but the role demands proficient C++ or Rust engineers who build low-latency routing data pipelines and geospatial tile transformation systems. His C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, neither of which relates to routing or navigation. While his GISCollective work demonstrates geospatial interest, it is an event-driven data collection tool for NGOs — not a routing tile pipeline. There is no evidence of experience with map data transformation, routing tile generation, navigation guidance systems, or the fault-tolerant distributed infrastructure this role requires.
strengths: Geospatial platform ownership via GISCollective demonstrates firsthand exposure to geo-data modeling and map-centric software design.

High-performance, low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, caching) shows familiarity with the scale and reliability demands of data pipelines.
Strong TDD and software craftsmanship practices indicate disciplined engineering habits that transfer well to quality-sensitive infrastructure work.
concerns: C++ experience is superficial and over a decade old (6-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015); no Rust experience is mentioned anywhere.
No exposure to routing, navigation guidance, or tile-based geospatial data pipelines — the GIS work is limited to NGO data collection, not production navigation infrastructure.
Career trajectory is firmly web/TypeScript-centric; shifting to a C++-heavy, systems-level routing pipeline role would require a significant and undemonstrated technical pivot.
